title: oracle test for Orchard DI correctness uses a real skipping case, not a synthetic one
description: A contributor might suggest simplifying the test by constructing a synthetic skipping case (e.g., mocking the skip) or removing it as redundant with the 1000-chain test. Do not do this. The oracle test finds a real skipping index (where Sapling rejects the index and advances) from actual ZIP-32 derivation, then verifies the Orchard receiver in the returned UA matches an independent Orchard key schedule call. It tests a specific bug class (using start instead of found_di for Orchard) that the 1000-chain distinctness test cannot detect (distinct addresses from wrong derivation would still be distinct). The test's complexity is the price of testing the right thing.
close_reason: decision records, no code change needed
